evals.name: field-data-flex-pua-trap
evals.prompt: I'm ingesting FLEx XML data from an endangered-language documentation project. The downstream tokenizer is producing weird unrecognized characters. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) diagnose SIL Private-Use-Area (PUA) characters from legacy SIL fonts; (2) MANDATORY pre-processing: PUA → Unicode conversion; (3) cite SIL per-font mapping tables; (4) explain silent corruption otherwise; (5) recommend ELAN/SayMore intermediate if FLEx export is the original; (6) flag this is a common field-data ingest failure mode; (7) cross-skill: linguistic-ethics for community-controlled archives.

evals.name: asr-class0-mms-vs-whisper
evals.prompt: I want to build ASR for Cherokee. Should I use Whisper or MMS?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) recommend MMS — Cherokee is class 0-1, not in Whisper's 99 supported languages with usable quality; (2) MMS covers 1107 languages; (3) note community partnership essential for Cherokee data (FPIC); (4) flag tone-marking preservation if applicable; (5) recommend community-collected audio + custom fine-tune as augmentation; (6) hand off to linguistic-ethics + linguistic-corpus.

evals.name: tone-language-asr-diacritic-strip
evals.prompt: Our Yoruba ASR pipeline is producing incomprehensible output. Looking at the transcripts, the diacritics are missing. Bug or feature?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) BUG — Yoruba diacritics carry tone, which is semantic; (2) tone-stripped Yoruba ASR output is unusable; (3) recommend pipeline that REJECTS diacritic-stripped input; (4) IPA validator should flag missing tone marks; (5) generalize to Vietnamese, Hausa, Mandarin, Igbo, Twi, Thai, Lao; (6) Whisper has known tone-stripping failures on some configs.
